question_title: What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-04-09?
summary: The 4-week Treasury bill rate (DTB4WK) is forecast to be very close to its current value of 3.64% on April 9, 2026. The rate has been extraordinarily stable throughout Q1 2026, fluctuating in a narrow 3.62-3.65% band during March, driven by the unchanged fed funds target range of 3.50-3.75%. With no FOMC meeting in April (next meeting in May), the policy anchor remains fixed. Monte Carlo simulation using the empirical discrete daily change distribution (±0.01 or 0) over 7 trading days produces a tight distribution centered on 3.64%. A 5% stress scenario weight accounts for potential macro disruptions from the ongoing Iran war, but even stress scenarios are constrained by the fed funds rate anchor for ultra-short T-bills.

factors.description: Current value is 3.64%, rate has been in tight 3.62-3.65 range all March 2026 with zero net drift
No FOMC meeting in April - fed funds target range stays at 3.50-3.75%, anchoring the 4-week T-bill near 3.64%
Daily changes are discrete (±0.01 or 0) with symmetric distribution and zero mean over 7 trading days
Iran war / oil shock macro uncertainty - but 4-week T-bill is anchored by short-term rate policy, not inflation expectations
Flight-to-safety risk could temporarily push T-bill yields below fed funds rate if crisis escalates
factors.conditional: If Iran war escalation triggers major flight-to-safety

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**4-Week Treasury Bill Rate on April 9, 2026: ~3.64%**

### Key Findings

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Current value (Mar 31)** | 3.64% |
| **Median forecast** | 3.64% |
| **P10–P90 range** | 3.62–3.66% |
| **P1–P99 range** | 3.60–3.68% |

### Rationale

The distribution is very tight because:

1. **Fed funds anchor**: The 4-week T-bill trades at virtual parity with the fed funds effective rate (3.64%), which is set by the 3.50-3.75% target range.

2. **No April FOMC meeting**: The next meeting is in May, so the policy rate is locked. This removes the primary source of variance for ultra-short T-bills.

3. **Empirical stability**: March 2026 data shows the rate fluctuated only ±1 basis point per day, staying within a 3bp range (3.62-3.65) for the entire month.

4. **Macro context**: While the Iran war creates broader uncertainty, ultra-short T-bills are driven by overnight rate policy rather than inflation expectations or long-term risk premia.

The Monte Carlo simulation uses the actual empirical discrete change distribution from March 2026 (±0.01 or 0 changes) over 7 trading days, with a 5% stress scenario weight for potential crisis-driven dislocations.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-04-09?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42992)
[4-Week Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DTB4WK)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series DTB4WK once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=DTB4WK`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-09 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.

factors.description: Current value is 3.64%, rate has been in tight 3.62-3.65 range all March
No FOMC meeting in April - fed funds target range stays at 3.50-3.75%, anchoring the 4-week T-bill
Daily changes are discrete (±0.01 or 0) with near-zero drift over 7 trading days
Iran war / oil shock creates macro uncertainty but 4-week T-bill is anchored by short-term rate policy, not long-term inflation expectations
Flight-to-safety could temporarily push T-bill yields down if crisis escalates
factors.conditional: If Iran war escalation triggers major flight-to-safety

input.anchor: 3.64% - current value, which equals the fed funds effective rate. With no FOMC meeting, this is the strongest anchor for ultra-short T-bills.
input.assessment: The 4-week T-bill rate is essentially pinned by the fed funds effective rate at 3.64%. With no FOMC meeting scheduled in April (next is May), the target range of 3.50-3.75% will remain unchanged. The rate has been extraordinarily stable throughout March 2026, fluctuating only in the 3.62-3.65 range with daily changes limited to ±1 basis point. Monte Carlo simulation using the empirical discrete change distribution over 7 trading days produces a very tight distribution centered on 3.64. The mixture model (95% base case + 5% stress scenario) still produces a narrow range because even stress scenarios are constrained by the fed funds anchor. The Iran war creates broader macro uncertainty but ultra-short T-bills are driven by monetary policy, not inflation expectations.
input.calibration_notes: Distribution is very tight (P10-P90 of 3.62-3.66) which accurately reflects the empirical reality: this rate has barely moved in 3 months. The Monte Carlo uses observed daily change frequencies, not assumed parameters. Width is consistent with random walk of discrete ±0.01 changes over 7 days.
input.key_uncertainties: Main uncertainty is whether some unusual market event (debt ceiling, extreme flight-to-safety, liquidity crunch) could push the rate outside its recent range. But even in such scenarios, the fed funds rate acts as a strong gravitational pull.
input.tool_audit: FRED data via fetch_url: excellent, provided full March daily data. Research sub-agent: very useful for context on Fed policy, Iran war, and macro backdrop. Monte Carlo simulation: straightforward and appropriate for this discrete data. No tool failures.
input.update_triggers: Emergency FOMC meeting (very unlikely), major Treasury market dislocation, sudden change in Treasury supply (debt ceiling resolution), extreme flight-to-safety event pushing yields sharply lower.
input.process_reflection: This question type (short-horizon rate forecast with no expected policy change) is well-served by the discrete Monte Carlo approach. The FRED data tool was excellent. The research agent provided valuable context confirming no April FOMC meeting. Process was smooth - the main challenge is correctly calibrating the tail width for an extremely stable series.
